ddls:
CREATE TABLE "labels" (
  "label_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each label */,
  "label_name" TEXT /* Name of the label */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the label */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("label_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "response_codes" (
  "code_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each HTTP response code */,
  "code" INTEGER /* HTTP response code */,
  "message" TEXT /* Associated message with the response code */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("code_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "threads" (
  "thread_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for thread usage data */,
  "request_id" INTEGER /* ID of the request */,
  "group_threads" INTEGER /* Number of active threads in the group */,
  "all_threads" INTEGER /* Total number of active threads */,
  "thread_name" TEXT /* Name of the thread */,
  "thread_state" TEXT /* State of the thread (e.g., running, waiting) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("thread_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "latency" (
  "latency_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for latency data */,
  "request_id" INTEGER /* ID of the request */,
  "latency" INTEGER /* Latency of the request */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("latency_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "idle_time" (
  "idle_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for idle time data */,
  "request_id" INTEGER /* ID of the request */,
  "idle_time" INTEGER /* Idle time before the request was processed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("idle_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "connect_time" (
  "connect_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for connection time data */,
  "request_id" INTEGER /* ID of the request */,
  "connect_time" INTEGER /* Time taken to establish the connection */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("connect_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, analyst) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last login */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the user is active */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the user was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the user's information */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "request_id" INTEGER /* ID of the request that triggered the alert */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who should receive the alert */,
  "alert_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of the alert */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of alert (e.g., performance degradation, system outage) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the alert */,
  "alert_severity" TEXT /* Severity level of the alert (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  "resolved" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the alert has been resolved */,
  "resolution_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the alert was resolved */,
  "resolution_notes" TEXT /* Notes on how the alert was resolved */,
  "syslog_id" TEXT /* ID of the syslog entry related to the alert */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_syslog_id FOREIGN KEY ("syslog_id") REFERENCES syslogs ("log_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "dashboards" (
  "dashboard_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each dashboard */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the dashboard */,
  "dashboard_name" TEXT /* Name of the dashboard */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the dashboard */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the dashboard was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the dashboard */,
  "is_shared" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the dashboard is shared with other users */,
  "shared_with" TEXT /* List of user IDs who have access to the shared dashboard */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("dashboard_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_dashboards_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "servers" (
  "server_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each server */,
  "server_name" TEXT /* Name of the server */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the server */,
  "location" TEXT /* Geographical location of the server */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the server (e.g., online, offline) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the server was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the server's information */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("server_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sessions" (
  "session_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for each session */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user associated with the session */,
  "start_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the session started */,
  "end_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the session ended */,
  "duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the session in seconds */,
  "location" TEXT /* Geographical location of the user during the session */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("session_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "client_devices" (
  "device_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each device */,
  "device_type" TEXT /* Type of device (e.g., mobile, desktop) */,
  "os" TEXT /* Operating system of the device */,
  "browser" TEXT /* Browser used by the client */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the device was first recorded */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the device's information */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "syslogs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the log entry */,
  "log_level" TEXT /* Level of the log (e.g., info, warning, error) */,
  "message" TEXT /* Log message */,
  "request_id" INTEGER /* ID of the request associated with the log entry */,
  "server_id" INTEGER /* ID of the server associated with the log entry */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id")
)
